pg69-p1-s1:
Then we have the voices who cry for sex and more sex; who bewail the institution of marriage; who think that most of the
troubles of the race are traceable to sex causes.
pg69-p1-s2:
They think we do not have enough of it, or that it isn't the right
kind.
pg69-p1-s4:
One school would allow man no flavor for his fare and the other would have
us all on a straight pepper diet.
pg69-p1-s5: We want to stay out of this controversy.
pg69-p1-s6:
We do not want to be the arbiter of anyone's
sex conduct.
pg69-p1-s8: We'd hardly be human if we didn't.
pg69-p1-s9: What can we do about them?
pg69-p1:
Then we have the voices who cry for sex and more sex; who bewail the institution of marriage; who think that most of the
troubles of the race are traceable to sex causes. They think we do not have enough of it, or that it isn't the right
kind. They see its significance everywhere. One school would allow man no flavor for his fare and the other would have
us all on a straight pepper diet. We want to stay out of this controversy. We do not want to be the arbiter of anyone's
sex conduct. We all have sex problems. We'd hardly be human if we didn't. What can we do about them?
pg69-p2-s1: We reviewed our own conduct over the years past.
pg69-p2-s2: Where had we been selfish, dishonest, or inconsiderate?
pg69-p2-s4: Did we unjustifiably arouse jealousy, suspicion or bitterness?
pg69-p2-s5:
Where were we at fault, what should we have done
instead?
pg69-p2-s6: We got this all down on paper and looked at it.
pg69-p2:
We reviewed our own conduct over the years past. Where had we been selfish, dishonest, or inconsiderate? Whom had we
hurt? Did we unjustifiably arouse jealousy, suspicion or bitterness? Where were we at fault, what should we have done
instead? We got this all down on paper and looked at it.
pg69-p3-s1: In this way we tried to shape a sane and sound ideal for our future sex life.
pg69-p3-s2:
We subjected each relation to this test -
was it selfish or not?
pg69-p3-s3: We asked God to mold our ideals and help us to live up to them.
pg69-p3-s4:
We remembered always that our sex
powers were God-given and therefore good, neither to be used lightly or selfishly nor to be despised and loathed.
pg69-p3:
In this way we tried to shape a sane and sound ideal for our future sex life. We subjected each relation to this test -
was it selfish or not? We asked God to mold our ideals and help us to live up to them. We remembered always that our sex
powers were God-given and therefore good, neither to be used lightly or selfishly nor to be despised and loathed.
pg69-p4-s1: Whatever our ideal turns out to be, we must be willing to grow toward it.
pg69-p4-s2:
We must be willing to make amends where we
have done harm, provided that we do not bring about still more harm in so doing.
pg69-p4-s3:
In other words, we treat sex as we
would any other problem.
pg69-p4-s4: In meditation, we ask God what we should do about each specific matter.
pg69-p4-s5:
The right answer will
come, if we want it.
pg69-p4:
Whatever our ideal turns out to be, we must be willing to grow toward it. We must be willing to make amends where we
have done harm, provided that we do not bring about still more harm in so doing. In other words, we treat sex as we
would any other problem. In meditation, we ask God what we should do about each specific matter. The right answer will
come, if we want it.
pg69-p5-s1: God alone can judge our sex situation.
